license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.65hhg36
title: Endometrium - Collagenase
author: Roser Vento-Tormo

steps_raw.instruction: Note: Flash-frozen tissue with isopentane for Spatial Transcriptomics work. {optional} Note: If tissue is going to be transported, do it with preservation solution (HypoThermosol® FRS) at 4 °C . Store sample for fixing in formalin (RNA Scope) & nuclei sequencing (flash-frozen) {optional}. Scrape off the blood vessels on remaining tissues. Note: We can skip this step if the donor is perfused.
Place wet tissue under a petri dish. Take 2 scalpels and roughly mince up the tissue. This step is crucial to increase the efficiency of the digestion.
Transfer contents to 50ml falcon containing the collagenase mix (~ 3 mL /tissue but it will depend on the size of the tissue)
Tighten lid and then seal with parafilm.
Incubate at 37 °C for 00:45:00 . Shacking during the incubation is recommended.
Filter sample through small strainer (100um) – do not discard retained tissue. The retained tissue will be used for "Endometrium-Trypsin" protocol.
Filtered material: Centrifuge at 450 g, 00:05:00 (0.5 rcf, 00:05:00 ).
Wash with 10 mL of PBS twice.
Resuspend with 1 mL RPMI 10%, count cells and sort for cell population (refer to "Cell staining for flow cytometry and sorting" protocol)

{optional- for biopsies we do this} Resuspend sample with 2 mL to 4 mL of 1X RBC lysis buffer mix and incubate for 00:10:00 . *RLB preparation: Dilute 10X RLB stock with water. After RBC lysis, add 10 mL of RPMI 10% and centrifuge 450g for 00:05:00 Wash twice with 10ml of PBS.
{Optional} Resuspend cells in freezing medium to a concentration of 1x 10 7 cells and aliquot into cryogenic storage vials.
